exercises.title: Add private and getter
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to make the field private and add a getter method.

blanks.hint: Access modifier that restricts to same class

hints: private restricts access to same class only
Getter returns the field's type
Inside the class, access field directly: return name

exercises.title: Trace setter validation
exercises.scenario: Predict what happens when setter rejects bad data.
exercises.expected_solution:
Caught: Negative balance not allowed
Balance: 1000.0

test_cases.expected:
Caught: Negative balance not allowed
Balance: 1000.0

hints: setBalance(-50) throws exception
Exception caught — error message printed
Balance was NOT updated (validation prevented assignment)
Balance is still the original 1000.0

exercises.title: Encapsulated Person class
exercises.scenario: Build a class with private fields and getters.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Person` class with:
- Private fields: name (String), age (int)
- Constructor takes both
- Getter for name (`getName`)
- Getter for age (`getAge`)

In main, create a Person and print using getters.

Expected output:
Name: Aisha
Age: 28
exercises.starter_code: // Build Person class with private fields and getters
hints: Fields are private
Getters: getName, getAge — public methods with no parameters
Return type matches field type
Use getters in main: p.getName()

exercises.title: Fix the private field access
exercises.scenario: Public code can't access private fields.
exercises.instructions:
This code doesn't compile. Use a getter to fix it (don't change the field to public).

Expected output:
Rex

bugs.issue: d.name is private — can't access from outside Dog class.
bugs.fix: Add a getName() method to Dog, then call d.getName()

hints: Compilation error: name has private access in Dog
Don't make name public — add a getter instead
getName() returns the private field
Use d.getName() in main

exercises.title: Class with setter and validation
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Temperature` class:
- Private field: celsius (double)
- Constructor sets initial value (no validation needed)
- Getter: getCelsius()
- Setter: setCelsius() — REJECTS values below -273.15 (absolute zero)

In main:
1. Create Temperature with 25
2. Set to 100 — works, print new value
3. Try to set -300 — catch and print error
4. Print final value

Expected output:
After set to 100: 100.0
Rejected: Temperature cannot be below absolute zero
Final: 100.0
exercises.starter_code: // Build Temperature class with validating setter

test_cases.expected_stdout:
After set to 100: 100.0
Rejected: Temperature cannot be below absolute zero
Final: 100.0

hints: Field is private double celsius
Setter checks < -273.15 (absolute zero)
Invalid values throw IllegalArgumentException
After rejected set: value stays unchanged (100.0)
Note: validation prevents the assignment from happening

exercises.title: Boolean getter with isXxx pattern
exercises.instructions:
Create a `LightSwitch` class:
- Private field: on (boolean), starts false
- Constructor: no args needed
- Boolean getter: `isOn()` — note: 'is' prefix for booleans
- Method `turnOn()` sets on=true
- Method `turnOff()` sets on=false

In main:
1. Create switch
2. Print 'On: false'
3. Turn on, print 'On: true'
4. Turn off, print 'On: false'

Expected output:
On: false
On: true
On: false
exercises.starter_code: // Build LightSwitch class with isOn() getter

test_cases.expected_stdout:
On: false
On: true
On: false

hints: Boolean fields default to false (no need to initialize)
Boolean getter uses 'is' prefix, not 'get': isOn() not getOn()
turnOn/turnOff are methods, not setters (named for clarity)
No-arg constructor isn't needed — Java provides it if no other constructor

exercises.title: Refactor public fields to encapsulated
exercises.scenario: Convert a public-fields class to encapsulated form.
exercises.instructions:
Refactor this Movie class:
- Make all fields private
- Add getters for all fields
- Add setters ONLY for rating (with validation: 0 <= rating <= 10)
- title and year should be read-only (no setters)
- Update main to use getters

Expected output:
Inception (2010): 8.8
After rating update: 9.0
Rejected: Rating must be between 0 and 10

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Inception (2010): 8.8
After rating update: 9.0
Rejected: Rating must be between 0 and 10

hints: Make all 3 fields private
Add getTitle, getYear, getRating
ONLY add setRating — title and year remain read-only
setRating validates: rating < 0 OR rating > 10 throws exception
main uses getters instead of direct field access

exercises.title: Encapsulated Counter
exercises.scenario: Class that exposes operations, not direct field access.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Counter` class:
- Private field: count (int), starts at 0
- Method `increment()` — adds 1
- Method `decrement()` — subtracts 1, but NEVER goes below 0
- Method `reset()` — sets to 0
- Getter: `getCount()`
- NO setter for count — only the operations can change it

In main:
- Increment 5 times
- Decrement 8 times (should stop at 0, not go negative)
- Print 'Count: 0'

Expected output:
Count: 0
exercises.starter_code:
// Build Counter class with controlled mutation
// No setCount — only the operations modify count
hints: Field private int count (defaults to 0)
increment: count++
decrement: only if count > 0 (prevent going negative)
No setter — count can only change through these methods
5 increments → 5, then 8 decrements but stops at 0
This is encapsulation FORCING valid state through limited operations

exercises.title: Fix the boolean getter name
exercises.instructions:
This code uses an unconventional name. Rename the getter to follow JavaBean convention.

Expected output:
Active: true
Active: false

bugs.issue: Boolean getter named getActive() — should be isActive() per JavaBean convention

hints: main calls u.isActive() — but the class only has getActive()
Compilation error: method isActive() not found
Boolean getters should use 'is' prefix
Rename getActive() → isActive()

exercises.title: Immutable Point class
exercises.scenario: Build an immutable class — no setters, all fields final.
exercises.instructions:
Create an IMMUTABLE `Point` class for 2D coordinates:
- Private FINAL fields: x (double), y (double)
- Constructor: takes x, y
- Getters: getX(), getY()
- NO setters
- Method `translate(double dx, double dy)` returns a NEW Point with shifted coords (doesn't modify this!)

In main:
- Create p1 at (3, 4)
- Get p2 = p1.translate(1, 2) → new point at (4, 6)
- Print both — p1 unchanged

Expected output:
p1: (3.0, 4.0)
p2: (4.0, 6.0)
p1 still: (3.0, 4.0)
exercises.starter_code:
// Build Point class as IMMUTABLE
// No setters! translate returns a NEW Point
hints: Fields: private final double x, y
Final means even the class can't reassign after constructor
translate doesn't change this object — creates and returns a NEW Point
This is how String works: every operation returns a new String
Original p1 stays at (3, 4) — immutability guarantees this

exercises.title: Read-only ID, settable name
exercises.instructions:
Create an `Employee` class:
- Private final field: id (int) — read-only forever
- Private fields: name (String), salary (double) — modifiable
- Constructor takes all three
- Getters for all three
- Setter for name (validates: non-empty)
- Setter for salary (validates: >= 0)
- NO setId method

In main:
1. Create employee #101 "Aisha" with salary 75000
2. Increase salary to 85000
3. Print info
4. Verify can't set id (commented out for clarity)

Expected output:
Employee #101 Aisha — ₹85000.0
hints: id is final — set once in constructor, never changes
No setId method — IDs shouldn't change after creation
name and salary have validating setters
All fields private, all access via methods

exercises.title: Computed property (no field)
exercises.scenario: Getter that computes a value instead of returning a field.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Circle` class:
- Private final field: radius (double)
- Constructor sets radius (validates: > 0)
- Getter: getRadius()
- 'getter' for area: `getArea()` — COMPUTES π × r², no underlying area field
- 'getter' for circumference: `getCircumference()` — COMPUTES 2 × π × r

In main:
- Create circle with radius 5
- Print radius, area, circumference

Expected output:
Radius: 5.0
Area: 78.53981633974483
Circumference: 31.41592653589793
exercises.starter_code:
// Circle class with computed area and circumference
// No 'area' or 'circumference' fields — compute them on demand
hints: Only ONE field: radius
getArea computes π × r × r on demand
getCircumference computes 2 × π × r
Math.PI is the constant
Computed properties avoid stale data — always reflects current radius

exercises.title: Password class (no getter!)
exercises.scenario: Encapsulation can also mean RESTRICTING reading.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Password` class for secure storage:
- Private field: hashedValue (String)
- Constructor: takes plain password, stores its 'hash' (use a simple transformation: reverse the string for this exercise — real apps use SHA-256)
- Method `matches(String plainAttempt)` — checks if reversed attempt equals stored hash
- NO getter for hashedValue (callers shouldn't see the hash directly)

In main:
1. Create password from "secret123"
2. Verify matches("secret123") returns true
3. Verify matches("wrong") returns false

Expected output:
Correct password? true
Wrong password? false
exercises.starter_code:
// Password class - the hash should NOT be retrievable
// Use reverse as our 'hash' function for this exercise

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Correct password? true
Wrong password? false

hints: Use a PRIVATE helper method hash() to do the 'hashing'
Reverse: new StringBuilder(s).reverse().toString() (callback to Topic 3.5!)
matches() hashes the attempt and compares with stored hash
Use .equals() for String comparison (not ==)
NO getter for hashedValue — that's the whole point
Real apps use SHA-256 or bcrypt; we use reverse for simplicity

exercises.title: Encapsulated BankAccount with operations
exercises.scenario: Real banking class — encapsulated, with deposit/withdraw operations.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `BankAccount` class:
- Private final field: accountNumber (int)
- Private fields: holderName (String), balance (double)
- Constructor: takes all three; validates accountNumber > 0, name non-empty, balance >= 0
- Getters for all three
- Setter for holderName (validates non-empty)
- NO setBalance — that would defeat the purpose!
- Method `deposit(double amount)` — validates > 0, adds to balance
- Method `withdraw(double amount)` — validates > 0 and <= balance, subtracts

In main:
1. Create account #1001 for Aisha with ₹5000
2. Deposit 2000 → balance 7000
3. Withdraw 1500 → balance 5500
4. Try withdraw 10000 (should fail)
5. Print final state

Expected output:
Account #1001 Aisha — ₹5500.0
exercises.starter_code:
// Build encapsulated BankAccount
// NO setBalance method! Only deposit and withdraw can modify balance
hints: accountNumber is final (never changes)
NO setBalance method — that's the encapsulation point
deposit validates amount > 0, adds to balance
withdraw validates amount > 0 AND amount <= balance
Failed withdraw doesn't change balance
5000 + 2000 - 1500 = 5500 (the 10000 attempt was rejected)

exercises.title: URL builder (immutable + fluent)
exercises.scenario: Immutable class with fluent interface — common production pattern.
exercises.instructions:
Create an immutable `URL` class:
- Private final fields: protocol, host, path (all String)
- Constructor takes all three (defaults each to empty string if null)
- Getters: getProtocol(), getHost(), getPath()
- Method `withProtocol(String p)` returns a NEW URL with new protocol (other fields unchanged)
- Method `withHost(String h)` returns a NEW URL with new host
- Method `withPath(String p)` returns a NEW URL with new path
- Method `toUrl()` returns formatted string

In main:
- Start with new URL("https", "google.com", "/")
- Chain: change path to '/search', then change host to 'bing.com'
- Print original (unchanged) and modified

Expected output:
Original: https://google.com/
Modified: https://bing.com/search
exercises.starter_code:
// Immutable URL with 'with' methods (fluent builder style)
// Each 'with' method returns a NEW URL with that change
hints: All fields private final
'with' methods create a NEW URL with one field changed
Chain: url.withPath(...).withHost(...) returns final URL
Original is NEVER modified — immutability
This pattern is used by HttpClient.Builder, ResponseBuilder, etc.
